content:
Learning IoT has been an exciting journey and every practical is helping me understand how software and hardware work together. 💻⚡

We started with the basics by creating different LED blinking patterns using the GPIO pins of Raspberry Pi. It was a simple practical but it gave me a clear understanding of how Raspberry Pi interacts with external components.

Our next practical was interfacing a TM1637 4-Digit 7-Segment Display with Raspberry Pi to display the current time. Writing the code, debugging errors, fixing the connections, and finally seeing the display work correctly was such a satisfying experience.

I'm genuinely enjoying these IoT practicals and this is just the beginning. Looking forward to learning more and building exciting things with Raspberry Pi! 🚀

And yes... the practical was successful, the output was correct, and I got a free lesson in electrical safety too. 😂⚡

comment: A useful next step is adding pull-up/pull-down handling and debouncing to your GPIO work. Those small details save a lot of head-scratching once you move beyond LEDs to real sensors.
